skill.name: css-animations
skill.description: CSS animation adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when authoring CSS keyframes, animation-delay based timing, animation-fill-mode, animation-play-state, or CSS-only motion that HyperFrames must seek deterministically during preview and rendering.
skill.summary: Guidance for writing seekable CSS keyframe animations inside HyperFrames compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: This skill is a Markdown documentation file describing CSS animation patterns for the HyperFrames video framework. All 26 static findings were evaluated as false positives. The detected backticks are Markdown code fences, the URLs are MDN documentation links, and there are no executable scripts, secrets, or malicious patterns.

content.user_title: Author Seekable CSS Animations for HyperFrames
content.value_statement: CSS animations in video pipelines often break when frames must be sampled at arbitrary times. This skill teaches Claude to write deterministic keyframe code that HyperFrames can seek frame by frame during preview and rendering.
actual_capabilities: Explains the HyperFrames CSS runtime adapter contract for seekable animations.
Provides a basic pulse-ring keyframe pattern with the required data attributes.
Provides a stagger pattern using CSS custom properties and animation-delay.
Lists property choices that work well with the adapter, such as transforms over layout properties.
Documents validation commands used to confirm a composition is well formed.
References the underlying adapter source path and MDN animation documentation.
limitations: Only covers CSS-based motion. Use the GSAP or WAAPI skills for full scene choreography.
Does not provide runnable scripts. The user must run the HyperFrames CLI themselves.
Assumes a HyperFrames project is already initialized.
Does not handle audio, video assets, or asset preprocessing.

use_cases.title: Add a decorative pulse loop to a clip
use_cases.description: A motion designer needs a pulsing ring overlay on a HyperFrames clip. Claude follows the basic pattern and produces a finite, seekable keyframe animation.

use_cases.title: Create a staggered dots intro
use_cases.description: A developer wants a row of dots that animate in sequence at the start of a clip. Claude uses the stagger pattern with CSS variables to avoid duplicate keyframes.

use_cases.title: Audit an existing composition for non-seekable motion
use_cases.description: A team lead asks Claude to review CSS in a composition and flag infinite animations, hover triggers, or layout property animations that will not seek correctly.

prompt_templates.title: Add a simple looping background animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the css-animations skill, add a slow shimmer background animation to my HyperFrames clip element. Use finite iteration count and animation-fill-mode both.

prompt_templates.title: Build a staggered entrance
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the css-animations skill, create a staggered fade-in for five icons inside a HyperFrames clip. Use CSS custom properties for delay and keep keyframes shared.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: stagger pattern with shared keyframes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor JS-driven motion to CSS
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my composition and convert any wall-clock JavaScript timers driving simple loops into CSS keyframe animations that HyperFrames can seek.

prompt_templates.title: Audit composition for seek safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit all CSS animations in my HyperFrames composition. Flag infinite durations, layout-property animations, and event-triggered motion. Suggest seekable replacements.

output_examples.input: Add a pulse-ring loop that runs three times over four seconds in my clip.
output_examples.output: Claude produces a clip div with data-start and data-duration attributes, a .pulse-ring style block with finite animation-duration and animation-iteration-count of three, and a keyframes block animating opacity and transform. The animation-fill-mode is set to both so the seeked frame is correct before and after the motion.

output_examples.input: Stagger three dots with a 120ms offset using one keyframe.
output_examples.output: Claude produces three span elements that each set a CSS variable for their index. The shared style uses animation-delay calculated from that variable, with one shared keyframe block. The result is a single seekable timeline that scales to more dots without duplicating keyframes.

best_practices: Prefer transform and opacity over layout properties so the browser can compose motion efficiently.
Always set animation-fill-mode to both so seeked frames hold the correct state at the edges.
Use finite animation-iteration-count values that cover the visible clip duration.
anti_patterns: Using infinite animations without confirming the browser exposes seekable WAAPI handles.
Triggering motion on hover, focus, scroll, or media queries that the renderer does not control.
Toggling animation classes after runtime startup without a deterministic timeline driving the change.

faq.question: When should I use CSS animations instead of GSAP in HyperFrames?
faq.answer: Use CSS for simple, single-element loops like shimmer or pulse. Use GSAP when motion must be sequenced across multiple elements.

faq.question: Why does animation-fill-mode matter for video rendering?
faq.answer: The renderer may seek to a frame before or after the active motion. Setting it to both ensures the element shows the correct state at those times.

faq.question: Can I use infinite animations?
faq.answer: Only if the browser supports seekable WAAPI-backed CSS animation handles. Otherwise use a finite iteration count that covers the visible duration.

faq.question: Why should I avoid animating top, left, width, or height?
faq.answer: These properties trigger layout work and are slower to render. Use transform and opacity when possible for smooth, seekable motion.

faq.question: How does HyperFrames seek a CSS animation?
faq.answer: The adapter finds elements with a computed animation-name and seeks the browser Animation handle, or pauses with a negative animation-delay as a fallback.

faq.question: How do I validate a composition after adding CSS animations?
faq.answer: Run the HyperFrames lint and validate commands from your project. They check the composition for adapter contract violations.
